display.purpose: Hero promise and section H2s. Barlow Condensed 900, uppercase, packed tight — the loudest voice.

lead.purpose: Section intros and About body — the warm, human register. DM Sans 1.125rem.

body.purpose: Default reading copy. DM Sans 1rem, 65–75ch measure.

label.purpose: Space Mono 0.7rem, 0.2em tracking, uppercase. Auto-untracks in Arabic.

shadows.name: glass-float
shadows.purpose: Hero/navbar CTAs floating over imagery only. The inset highlight sells the glass.

motion.name: reveal-rise
motion.purpose: Scroll fade-and-rise (Reveal): y 24→0, opacity 0→1, 0.6s. Needs a reduced-motion crossfade.

motion.name: hero-spring
motion.purpose: Hero panel slam from opposite edges to the center seam.

components.name: Glass CTA
components.description: Primary call-to-action floating over hero imagery; midnight glass with ember hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-glass { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:12px; border-radius:9999px; border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.15); background:rgba(10,15,30,0.5); padding:16px 28px; font-family:'DM Sans',system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.75rem; font-weight:500; letter-spacing:0.1em; text-transform:uppercase; color:#fff; text-decoration:none; -webkit-backdrop-filter:blur(12px) saturate(1.5); backdrop-filter:blur(12px) saturate(1.5); box-shadow:0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.25), inset 0 1px 1px rgba(255,255,255,0.3); transition:background .3s ease; } .ds-btn-glass:hover { background:rgba(255,77,0,0.4); } .ds-btn-glass:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #ff4d00; outline-offset:3px; }

components.name: Accent Button
components.css: .ds-btn-accent { border:none; border-radius:9999px; background:#ff4d00; color:#fff; padding:16px 32px; font-family:'Barlow Condensed',system-ui,sans-serif; font-weight:700; font-size:1rem; letter-spacing:0.1em; text-transform:uppercase; cursor:pointer; transition:background .2s ease; } .ds-btn-accent:hover { background:rgba(255,77,0,0.9); } .ds-btn-accent:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #141414; outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Eyebrow Label
components.description: Mono tracked uppercase section kicker with leading ember rule.
components.html: WHAT I DO
components.css: .ds-eyebrow { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:12px; } .ds-eyebrow__rule { display:inline-block; height:2px; width:24px; background:#ff4d00; } .ds-eyebrow__text { font-family:'Space Mono',monospace; font-size:0.7rem; letter-spacing:0.2em; text-transform:uppercase; color:#6b6b6b; }

components.name: Display Heading
components.description: Section H2 with single ember accent word.
components.html: Systems that scale without the chaos.
components.css: .ds-display { margin:0; font-family:'Barlow Condensed',system-ui,sans-serif; font-weight:900; text-transform:uppercase; font-size:clamp(2.5rem,6vw,5.25rem); line-height:0.95; letter-spacing:-0.02em; color:#141414; text-wrap:balance; max-width:18ch; } .ds-display__accent { color:#ff4d00; }

components.name: Service Card
components.description: Flat card in a hairline gap-px grid; ember top-rule scales in on hover.
components.html:
01

Custom Apps & Websites

Phone apps and websites built for your customers, not just for show.
components.css: .ds-card-service { position:relative; overflow:hidden; background:#faf9f6; padding:40px; } .ds-card-service__rule { position:absolute; inset-inline:0; top:0; height:2px; background:#ff4d00; transform:scaleX(0); transform-origin:left; transition:transform .3s ease; } .ds-card-service:hover .ds-card-service__rule { transform:scaleX(1); } .ds-card-service__num { display:block; font-family:'Barlow Condensed',system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:3rem; line-height:1; color:rgba(255,77,0,0.3); margin-bottom:24px; } .ds-card-service__title { margin:0 0 16px; font-family:'Barlow Condensed',system-ui,sans-serif; font-weight:700; text-transform:uppercase; font-size:1.875rem; line-height:1.1; color:#141414; transition:color .2s ease; } .ds-card-service:hover .ds-card-service__title { color:#ff4d00; } .ds-card-service__desc { margin:0; font-family:'DM Sans',system-ui,sans-serif; color:#6b6b6b; line-height:1.6; }

components.name: Underline Input
components.description: Borderless underline field on the midnight surface; underline turns ember on focus.
components.css: .ds-input { width:100%; background:transparent; border:none; border-bottom:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.12); padding:16px 0; color:#f5f5f5; font-family:'DM Sans',system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:1rem; outline:none; transition:border-color .2s ease; } .ds-input::placeholder { color:#9aa0ad; } .ds-input:focus { border-bottom-color:#ff4d00; }

components.name: Tag Chip
components.css: .ds-chip { display:inline-block; border:1px solid #e6e3dc; border-radius:9999px; padding:4px 12px; font-family:'DM Sans',system-ui,sans-serif; font-size:0.75rem; color:#6b6b6b; }

narrative.overview: The storefront of a maker who happens to be technical — a window into a workshop where real things get built for real businesses. The system runs on a deliberate two-surface split: a warm, paper-toned daylight surface where the work is shown plainly, and a deep midnight surface where the brand turns up the volume at the moments that ask for commitment. The voice is bold in execution, human in tone: Barlow Condensed set black/uppercase/tight carries the hierarchy, and ember-orange is used like a highlighter, never like paint.
keyCharacteristics: Two committed surfaces — warm paper daylight + midnight-navy spotlight — never a muddy middle gray.
Condensed, black, uppercase display type carrying the hierarchy; the page talks in headlines.
Ember accent rationed to ≤10% of any surface; rarity is the point.
Bilingual by design: Latin condensed/tracked, Arabic (Cairo) untracked and upright — both first-class.
Flat by default; depth appears only as glass on floating controls and hairline tonal grids.

rules.name: The Highlighter Rule
rules.body: Ember marks, it never fills areas. One accent word per headline, one rule per label, one filled button per view.

rules.name: The No-Middle-Gray Rule
rules.body: Every surface commits to paper or midnight. Drama comes from the jump between the two, not from a dimmer switch.

rules.name: The Headline-Is-Loudest Rule
rules.body: Display type is the largest thing on any surface, including the hero. No body or UI element out-scales a display heading in its own viewport.

rules.name: The One-Word-Ember Rule
rules.body: Inside a headline, exactly one word takes ember — the verb or the payoff, never a connector.

rules.name: The Arabic-Is-Native Rule
rules.body: Arabic runs at Cairo's natural width, tracking 0, no uppercase, with direction flipped — except the fixed-composition hero, which stays LTR.

rules.name: The Glass-Is-Earned Rule
rules.body: Backdrop-blur and the glass shadow are reserved for controls floating over photography. Forbidden as decoration on flat sections.

rules.name: The Flat-Grid Rule
rules.body: Card groups are separated by gap-px over a hairline background, not by shadows. The dividing line is the elevation.

dos: Do keep the hero headline the single largest type on the page — display scale, never smaller than the section H2s beneath it.
Do use the .eyebrow (Space Mono, 0.2em tracking, uppercase) for labels on Latin surfaces; trust it to auto-untrack in Arabic.
Do ration ember to ≤10% of a surface.
Do commit every surface to paper or midnight; carry warmth through type and accent, not the background.
Do cap body and lead copy at 65–75ch and keep body ≥16px.
Do add prefers-reduced-motion alternatives to every entrance, the hero spring, and Reveal.
Do reference the --accent token (#ff4d00), never a hardcoded hex.
donts: Don't ship the saturated creative-studio template — no oversized centered serif hero, no logo marquee, no identical project grid, no 'multidisciplinary studio' voice.
Don't ship the generic SaaS look — no gradient-blob hero, no three identical feature cards, no Inter/Roboto, no purple-to-blue gradient, no hero-metric template.
Don't drift to cheap-freelancer or corporate-sterile registers.
Don't overdesign; effects must earn their place.
Don't use gradient text or side-stripe borders.
Don't let a second ember element compete in the same glance, and don't let UI out-scale the display heading.
Don't lighten --muted-foreground past #6b6b6b on paper, or let midnight-muted placeholders drop below 4.5:1.
Don't hardcode #FF4500; it's drift from the #ff4d00 token.
